abstract.text: An interval from a lower bound up to, and including, an upper bound.
Create a new range using the closed range operator (

inlineContent.text: You create a
instance by using the closed range
operator (
A
instance contains both its lower bound and its
upper bound.
Because a closed range includes its upper bound, a closed range whose lower
bound is equal to the upper bound contains that value. Therefore, a
instance cannot represent an empty range.

content.text: Using a Closed Range as a Collection of Consecutive Values

inlineContent.text: When a closed range uses integers as its lower and upper bounds, or any
other type that conforms to the
protocol with an integer
stride, you can use that range in a
loop or with any sequence or
collection method. The elements of the range are the consecutive values
from its lower bound up to, and including, its upper bound.
Because floating-point types such as
and
are their own
types, they cannot be used as the bounds of a countable range. If
you need to iterate over consecutive floating-point values, see the
function.

A type that can decode itself from an external representation.
A type whose metatype can be shared across arbitrary concurrent contexts
without introducing a risk of data races.
A type that provides sequential, iterated access to its elements.
A collection that supports backward as well as forward traversal.
Returns a Boolean value indicating whether this range and the given range
contain an element in common.
Returns a Boolean value indicating whether two ranges are equal.
A Boolean value indicating whether the range contains no elements.
Now that Range is conditionally a collection when Bound: Strideable,
CountableRange is no longer needed. This is a deprecated initializer
for any remaining uses of Range(countableRange).
A thread-safe type whose values can be shared across arbitrary concurrent
contexts without introducing a risk of data races. Values of the type may
have no shared mutable state, or they may protect that state with a lock or
by forcing it to only be accessed from a specific actor.
Build apps using a powerful open language.
